\boxed{\text{Photoreceptor cells in the retina (e.g., rods and cones)}}

This occurs because transducin activation by light-absorbed rhodopsin stimulates phosphodiesterase (PDE) to hydrolyze cGMP, closing cGMP-gated cation channels and hyperpolarizing the cell. The most common example is **rods**, where this cascade reduces glutamate release, signaling visual transduction. Cones also share this mechanism but are less frequently emphasized in basic descriptions.
